g.text: SETUP MODE — logging only, nothing is blocked
Script interpreters like python3 and perl run arbitrary code files. If the
interpreter itself were allowlisted, every script it runs would inherit its
permissions — creating a broad attack surface.
HeartSuite solves this with Secure Script Launchers: small programs that sit
in front of the real interpreter, identify which script is being run, and
apply that script's own narrow permission set instead.
Redirects each interpreter through its Secure Script Launcher. After
activation, each script that runs will be reviewed on its own terms.
Interpreters without a launcher (bash, sh) are handled in the Programs queue.
What the log event count means:
The number of log events recorded for that interpreter. A high count
means it is heavily used — this is normal for system tools.
2 launcher(s) available but not yet activated.
Activate to redirect interpreters through their Secure Script Launchers.
